name: CFSCOPE
description: Floating scope with independent window for signal visualization

realParameters.description: Time offset, xmin, xmax, refresh period

integerParameters.description: Display config: ID, auto-flag, buffer, num_curves, colors[8], style, Y-limits, window_size, grid

controls.description: Event trigger for display refresh and data sampling

xmlExample:
<BasicBlock id="b1" parent="0:2:0" interfaceFunctionName="CFSCOPE" blockType="c" dependsOnU="1" dependsOnT="0" simulationFunctionName="cfscope" simulationFunctionType="C_OR_FORTRAN" style="CFSCOPE">
  <ScilabString as="exprs" height="9" width="1">
    <data line="0" column="0" value="1 3 5 7 9 11 13 15"/>
    <data line="1" column="0" value="-1"/>
    <data line="2" column="0" value="[]"/>
    <data line="3" column="0" value="[600;400]"/>
    <data line="4" column="0" value="-15"/>
    <data line="5" column="0" value="15"/>
    <data line="6" column="0" value="30"/>
    <data line="7" column="0" value="2"/>
    <data line="8" column="0" value="1"/>
  </ScilabString>
  <mxGeometry as="geometry" x="300.0" y="200.0" width="40.0" height="40.0"/>
</BasicBlock>
anomalies: No explicit input port - receives data via control event
ControlPort serves dual purpose: refresh AND data input
criticalRules: Event carries implicit data value (not just trigger)
Floating window independent of main diagram
differs from CSCOPE in port structure
